mnemonics: F-Strings Are Super Fast & Fun (F.A.S.F.F. for f"{string}")
Dot Format: Placeholder Period (The . is your placeholder for .format())
Old School Percent: Like C, But Cumbersome (Remember the % syntax comes from C)
keyPoints: f-strings (f"Hello {name}") are the modern, preferred method for string interpolation, offering clarity and speed.
The .format() method uses placeholders like {} and {} to insert values in order or by keyword.
The older %-formatting (e.g., "Hello %s" % name) uses format specifiers like %s for strings and %d for integers, similar to the C programming language.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the 'f' prefix before an f-string literal, resulting in literal curly braces in the output.
Mismatching the number of arguments or keywords in the .format() method with the number of placeholders in the string.
Using the wrong format specifier with %-formatting (e.g., using %d for a string), which causes a TypeError.
